title: Three-Dimensional Quantification of Spheroid Degradation-Dependent Invasion and Invadopodia Formation
author: Goertzen, Eymael, et al.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: In a T75 culture flask, culture tumor cell monolayers in cell medium (Dulbecco’s Modified Eagle Medium (DMEM) supplemented with 10% (v/v) fetal bovine serum (FBS)) in culture incubator (at 37 °C with 5% CO2) until cell confluency of 60–80%. In Fig. 1, human breast adenocarcinoma cell line, MDA-MB-231, and oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC) cell line, UMSCC1, were used.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Using phosphate buffered saline (PBS), wash tumor cell monolayers to remove culture medium (10 ml per 75 cm2).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Trypsinize cells (2 ml per 75 cm2) until cells have detached (4–10 min). Once cells have begun lifting off flask, add DMEM medium contacting 10% (v/v) FBS (8 ml) to inactivate trypsin. Transfer cell suspension into 15 ml conical tube.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cell suspension at 300 RCF for 5 min.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Aspirate cell medium supernatant. Using a P1000 pipette, re-suspend cell pellet in 1 ml of DMEM medium contacting 10% (v/v) FBS.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: With a hemocytometer, determine cell concentration of cell suspension. Dilute cell suspension with DMEM medium contacting 10% (v/v) FBS to obtain a concentration of 50,0000 cells/ ml.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Transfer 5,000 cells (100 μl) from cell suspension to each well of a Corning spheroid round bottom, Ultra-Low Attachment 96-well microplate.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Transfer 96-well microplate to cell culture incubator (at 37 °C with 5% CO2) and incubate cells until spheroids form (approximately 24–72 h).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Preparation: cool pipette tips (0.1–2.0 μl, 2–200 μl, 100–1000 μl) and 96-well flat bottom plate at 4 °C overnight.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Thaw Geltrex™ basement membrane matrix on ice until Geltrex™ is liquid (45–60 min).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Prepare 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tubes that will be used for each spheroid condition by labeling and placing on ice until cool.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: When Geltrex™ is liquid, distribute Geltrex™ into 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tubes for each condition of interest so that each spheroid well will contain 100 μl of Geltrex™. In other words, if the condition of interest will have 3 spheroids, distribute 300 μl of Geltrex™ into designated microcentrifuge tube for that condition using chilled pipette tips.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: At desired time (s) from initial placement of spheroids in Geltrex™, gently remove 100 μl of cell culture medium and replace with 100 μl of Leibowitz’s L-15 phenol red free medium supplemented with 10% (v/v) fetal bovine serum.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wrap periphery of 96-well plate with plastic paraffin film to ensure sterility.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Using confocal microscopy, locate and image the spheroid of each well. Images are taken with bright field channel and 505 nm excitation to visualize Green-BSA 515 nm emission signaling produced from spheroid Geltrex™ degradation. Utilizing z-stack image acquisition (1 μm slices), create a stack of images from the bottom of the spheroid to the top using Green-BSA signaling as measure.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove paraffin film and replace L-15 phenol red free medium with 100 μl of cell culture medium containing desired ligands or inhibitors. Incubate spheroids at 37 °C with 5% CO2.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Repeat previous steps (3.3.1–3.3.4) at each desired time points of experimentation to visualize spheroid growth and degradation (Fig. 1).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: To analyze spheroid growth and degradation volume, use image analysis software (Volocity 6.3). To measure total spheroid volume, use bright-field images and set software parameters to trace total area of spheroid for each image of the z-stack (Fig. 2a). Similarly, to measure total Geltrex™ degradation volume, use 515 nm images and set software parameters to measure areas of green fluoresce signal for each image of the z-stack. The spheroid volume and Geltrex™ degradation volume is calculated by the software as a single μm3 value per z-stack and can be reported for each day for comparison (Fig. 2b and c). Alternatively, if initial sizes of spheroids vary, volume of Geltrex™ degradation can be normalized to the first day of volume acquisition (Fig. 2d).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: At desired time from initial placement of spheroids in step 3.2.11, gently remove 100 μl of cell culture medium using P200.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash each well with PBS (100 μl).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Add 150 μl of room temperature 3.7% paraformaldehyde to each well and place 96-well plate at 4 °C overnight.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove paraformaldehyde using P200 and wash each well with PBS (100 μl) three-times. Add 100 μl of PBS and store at 4 °C until proceeding with step 3.4.5.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Remove PBS and dehydrate tissue using 100 μl per well in sequence of the following solutions: 50% ethanol (10 min), 70% ethanol (10 min), 80% ethanol (10 min), 95% ethanol (10 min), 100% ethanol (10 min), 100% ethanol (10 min), and 100% ethanol (10 min).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Transfer dehydrated Geltrex™ containing spheroid to 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Exchange ethanol with xylene using 100 μl per well in sequence of the following solutions: 2:1 ethanol: xylene (15 min), 1:1 ethanol: xylene (15 min), 1:2 ethanol: xylene (15 min), 100% xylene (15 min), 100% xylene (15 min), and 100% xylene (15 min).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Exchange xylene with paraffin, in paraffin oven set for 54–58 °C, using 100 μl per well in sequence of the following solutions: 2:1 xylene: paraffin (30 min), 1:1 xylene: paraffin (30 min), 1:2 xylene: paraffin (30 min), 100% paraffin (2 h), and 100% paraffin (overnight).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Embed in new paraffin and orient tissue in paraffin cassette for microtome sectioning.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Chill paraffin-embedded tissue blocks on ice before sectioning.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Fill a water bath with ultrapure water (45 °C).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Following the microtome manufacturer’s instructions, insert paraffin block and section at thickness of 5 μm.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Place sections in water bath to float and lift individual sections out of water bath using glass microscope slide.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Dry slides overnight at 37 °C.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: With selected slides, wash with TRIS-buffered saline (TBS) plus 0.025% Triton X-100 for 5 min in gentle slide shaker, repeat three-times.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Block with 1% (Bovine serum albumin) BSA in TBS for 2 h at room temperature.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Apply primary antibody diluted in TBS plus 1% BSA overnight at 4 °C. For invadopodia formation analysis, use primary antibodies for cortactin, Tks5, and MMP-14.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash slides with TBS plus 0.025% Triton X-100 for 5 min in gentle slide shaker, repeat three-times.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Apply secondary antibody diluted in TBS plus 1% BSA overnight at 4 °C.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash slides with TBS plus 0.025% Triton X-100 for 5 min in gentle slide shaker, repeat three-times.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Counterstain with DAPI (1 μg/ml) in TBS plus 1% BSA for 60 min at room temperature. Cover from ambient light.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash slides with TBS plus 0.025% Triton X-100 for 5 min in gentle slide shaker, repeat three-times.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Mount using mounting medium and add coverslip. Dry at room temperature for 24 h before imaging.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Using confocal microscopy, locate and image the spheroid of each slide section. Images are taken with 488 nm, 568 nm, 647 nm, and 405 nm excitation to visualize MMP-14, Cortactin, Tks5, and DAPI, respectively. Utilizing z-stack image acquisition (0.1 μm slices), create a stack of images from the bottom to top of the spheroid section for invadopodia analysis.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: To analyze invadopodia formation and maturation, use an image analysis software capable of handling 3D stacks (e.g. ZEISS ZEN 2.3, Imaris or Volocity 6.3). Co-localization of invadopodia markers cortactin and Tks5 (Fig. 3) are an indication for precursor invadopodia formation. Co-localization of cortactin and MMP-14 are representative of mature, invading invadopodia (Fig. 3).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: For spheroid visualization, hematoxylin and eosin (H & E) staining can be performed concurrently following step 3.4.14.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Using unstained spheroid section adjacent to section used for step 3.4.15, deparaffinize section using 100% xylene (3 min), repeat three times.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Re-hydrate in sequence 100% ethanol (2 min, three times), 95% ethanol (2 min, twice), and 70% ethanol (2 min, twice).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash in distilled water (2 min, twice).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Place slide in Harris Hematoxylin solution (7 min).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash in distilled water (2 min).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Differentiate in 0.5–1% acid alcohol (3 dips).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash in distilled water (1 min).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Counterstain in Lithium carbonate solution (1 min).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Wash in distilled water (2 min, twice)

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Place in 5% Eosin solution (1 dip)

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Dehydrate in sequence 95% ethanol (30 s, twice) and 100% ethanol (30 s, twice).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Place in 100% xylene (2 min, three times).

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Mount using 2–3 drops of mounting medium and add coverslip. Dry at room temperature for 24 h before imaging.

steps.title: Methods
steps.instruction: Image H&E stained spheroid section using light microscopy (Fig. 3).

steps.title: Spheroid Geltrex™ Degradation Analysis
steps.instruction: Leibowitz’s L-15 Medium, no phenol red (21083027, ThermoFisher) supplemented with 10% (v/v) fetal bovine serum. L-15 medium is buffered by phosphates and free base amino acids instead of sodium bicarbonate, alleviating the need for 5% CO2 during the live cell imaging. The absence of phenol red in L-15 is used to prevent phenol red autofluorescence during immunofluorescence image acquisition. Plastic paraffin film (Parafilm). Cell culture growth media (Dulbecco’s Modified Eagle Medium (DMEM; Thermo Fisher Scientific) supplemented with 10% (v/v) fetal bovine serum (FBS; Thermo Fisher Scientific), non-essential amino acids (100 nmol/L; Thermo Fisher Scientific), penicillin and streptomycin liquid (100 mg/mL; Thermo Fisher Scientific). Ligands and inhibitors (#8902sf, Cell signaling) and GM6001 (M5939 Millipore)). Spinning disk confocal microscopy (Quorum spinning disk confocal, Leica DMIRE2). We used Volocity 6.3 to analyze the 3D images generated from the spheroids. Using Volocity we were able to accurately select the BODIPY-green signal using the standard deviation of the pixel intensity and analyze volume, intensity, shape (circularity, roundness).

steps.title: Immunohistochemistry Spheroid Processing, Staining and Invadopodia Analysis
steps.instruction: 4% Paraformaldehyde (PFA). Phosphate Buffered Saline (PBS). Ethanol (50%, 70%, 80%, 95%, 100%). Ethanol: Xylene (2:1, 1:1; 1:2). Xylene (100%). Xylene: Paraffin (2:1, 1:1, 1:2). Paraffin (100%). Paraffin oven. Paraffin cassettes. Rotary Microtome (Leica RM2125 RTS). Superfrost Plus Microscope Slides 15x75x1.0 mm (Fisherbrand, # 12–550 15). TRIS-buffered saline (TBS) plus 0.025% Triton X-100. 1% (Bovine serum albumin) BSA in TBS. Primary Antibodies (Cortactin (ab84208, Abcam), Tks5 (ab118575, Abcam Biotechnology), MMP-14 (ab56307, Abcam). Secondary Antibodies (Donkey F(ab’)2 Anti-Goat IgG H&L (Alexa Fluor 647) (ab150139, Abcam), Donkey F(ab’)2 Anti-Rabbit IgG H&L (Alexa fluoro-568) (ab175694, Abcam), Donkey F(ab’)2 Anti-Mouse IgG H&L 488 (ab150155, Abcam). DAPI (NucBlue Fixed Cell Ready Probes reagent, Life technologies - Catalog# R37606). Distilled water. Harris Hematoxylin solution. Lithium carbonate solution. 5% Eosin solution. Mounting medium (ProLong diamond Antifade Mount, Life technologies, #P36961). Coverslips (22mmX50mm 1.5 oz. cover class, Electron Microscopy Sciences Sciences. Catalog#72204–04. ZEISS ELYRA PS.1 super-resolution microscopy, Quorum Spinning Disk Confocal microscope. Imaging analyzing software: ZEISS ZEN 2.3 and Volocity 6.3.

Paraffin (100%). Paraffin oven. Paraffin cassettes. Rotary Microtome (Leica RM2125 RTS). Superfrost Plus Microscope Slides 15x75x

steps.instruction: Block with 1% (Bovine serum albumin) BSA in TBS for 2 h at room temperature.

steps.title: Immunohistochemistry, Spheroid Processing, Staining and Invadopodia Analysis
steps.instruction: Apply primary antibody diluted in TBS plus 1% BSA overnight at 4 °C. For invadopodia formation analysis, use primary antibodies for cortactin, Tks5, and MMP-14.

steps.title: Immunohistochemistry, Spheroid Processing, Staining and Invadopodia Analysis
steps.instruction: Wash slides with TBS plus 0.025% Triton X-100 for 5 min in gentle slide shaker, repeat three-times.

steps.title: Immunohistochemistry, Spheroid Processing, Staining and Invadopodia Analysis
steps.instruction: Apply secondary antibody diluted in TBS plus 1% BSA overnight at 4 °C.

steps.title: Immunohistochemistry, Spheroid Processing, Staining and Invadopodia Analysis
steps.instruction: Wash slides with TBS plus 0.025% Triton X-100 for 5 min in gentle slide shaker, repeat three-times.

steps.title: Immunohistochemistry, Spheroid Processing, Staining and Invadopodia Analysis
steps.instruction: Counterstain with DAPI (1 μg/ml) in TBS plus 1% BSA for 60 min at room temperature. Cover from ambient light.

steps.title: Immunohistochemistry, Spheroid Processing, Staining and Invadopodia Analysis
steps.instruction: Wash slides with TBS plus 0.025% Triton X-100 for 5 min in gentle slide shaker, repeat three-times.

steps.title: Immunohistochemistry, Spheroid Processing, Staining and Invadopodia Analysis
steps.instruction: Mount using mounting medium and add coverslip. Dry at room temperature for 24 h before imaging.

steps.title: Immunohistochemistry, Spheroid Processing, Staining and Invadopodia Analysis
steps.instruction: Using confocal microscopy, locate and image the spheroid of each slide section. Images are taken with 488 nm, 568 nm, 647 nm, and 405 nm excitation to visualize MMP-14, Cortactin, Tks5, and DAPI, respectively. Utilizing z-stack image acquisition (0.1 μm slices), create a stack of images from the bottom to top of the spheroid section for invadopodia analysis.

steps.title: Immunohistochemistry, Spheroid Processing, Staining and Invadopodia Analysis
steps.instruction: To analyze invadopodia formation and maturation, use an image analysis software capable of handling 3D stacks (e.g. ZEISS ZEN 2.3, Imaris or Volocity 6.3). Co-localization of invadopodia markers cortactin and Tks5 (Fig. 3) are an indication for precursor invadopodia formation. Co-localization of cortactin and MMP-14 are representative of mature, invading invadopodia (Fig. 3).

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: For spheroid visualization, hematoxylin and eosin (H & E) staining can be performed concurrently following step 3.4.14.Using unstained spheroid section adjacent to section used for step 3.4.15, deparaffinize section using 100% xylene (3 min), repeat three times.Re-hydrate in sequence 100% ethanol (2 min, three times), 95% ethanol (2 min, twice), and 70% ethanol (2 min, twice).Wash in distilled water (2 min, twice).Place slide in Harris Hematoxylin solution (7 min).Wash in distilled water (2 min).Differentiate in 0.5–1% acid alcohol (3 dips).Wash in distilled water (1 min).Counterstain in Lithium carbonate solution (1 min).Wash in distilled water (2 min, twice)Place in 5% Eosin solution (1 dip)Dehydrate in sequence 95% ethanol (30 s, twice) and 100% ethanol (30 s, twice).Place in 100% xylene (2 min, three times).Mount using 2–3 drops of mounting medium and add coverslip. Dry at room temperature for 24 h before imaging.Image H&E stained spheroid section using light microscopy (Fig. 3).

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: For spheroid visualization, hematoxylin and eosin (H & E) staining can be performed concurrently following step 3.4.14.

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Using unstained spheroid section adjacent to section used for step 3.4.15, deparaffinize section using 100% xylene (3 min), repeat three times.

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Re-hydrate in sequence 100% ethanol (2 min, three times), 95% ethanol (2 min, twice), and 70% ethanol (2 min, twice).

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Wash in distilled water (2 min, twice).

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Place slide in Harris Hematoxylin solution (7 min).

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Wash in distilled water (2 min).

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Differentiate in 0.5–1% acid alcohol (3 dips).

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Wash in distilled water (1 min).

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Counterstain in Lithium carbonate solution (1 min).

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Wash in distilled water (2 min, twice)

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Place in 5% Eosin solution (1 dip)

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Dehydrate in sequence 95% ethanol (30 s, twice) and 100% ethanol (30 s, twice).

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Place in 100% xylene (2 min, three times).

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Mount using 2–3 drops of mounting medium and add coverslip. Dry at room temperature for 24 h before imaging.

steps.title: Hematoxylin and eosin staining
steps.instruction: Image H&E stained spheroid section using light microscopy (Fig. 3).
